How Much Does A Boeing 777X Engine Cost?

The largest and most powerful commercial engine ever built, the GE9X is by no means cheap

The GE9X engine's premium pricing reflects its cutting-edge technology and limited production scale, directly impacting 777X aircraft economics and airline fleet decisions.

Damaged Runway Lights Lead To Potential Fatal Error On United Airlines Flight In Chicago

A United Airlines Boeing 737-700 Causes Damage To Runway Lights At ORD After Reportedly Incorrectly Lining Up With Runway Incorrectly.

Runway infrastructure failures can compound pilot errors; proactive airfield maintenance and enhanced approach monitoring are critical safety measures.

Delta Air Lines Passenger Yells Racial Slur At Flight Attendant, Faces Up To 20 Years In Prison

Delta passenger may face prison sentence after allegedly using a racial slur against a flight attendant, highlighting ongoing unruly behavior risks.

Escalating federal penalties for onboard misconduct underscore airlines' zero-tolerance policies and crew safety priorities in managing passenger behavior.

Regulatory Mar 22, 2026

Here’s Why Southwest Airlines Can’t Fly All Of Its Boeing 737 MAX 8s Over The Pacific

These jets are subject to certain operational requirements.

Southwest's Pacific 737 MAX 8 restrictions stem from ETOPS certification gaps, limiting long-range operations until Boeing achieves required extended-range approval.

Military Mar 22, 2026

The Tomcat’s Six Pack: The F-14 Six on Six Phoenix Test

Designed for fleet air defense, the F-14 was armed with a potent long-range missile and, with a powerful new radar, could engage multiple airborne targets at once. The United States Navy’s F-14 Tomcat was the most potent fleet defense aircraft of its time. Its AIM-54 Phoenix missile had a long ra...

The F-14 Tomcat's simultaneous multi-target engagement capability via AIM-54 Phoenix missiles established the modern standard for naval fleet defense aircraft architecture.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 22, 2026

U.S. Army Receives DARPA’s Optionally Piloted H-60Mx Black Hawk

The U.S. Army will put the H-60Mx and Sikorsky’s MATRIX flight autonomy software through a rigorous operational testing campaign with real world logistics in contested scenarios. The U.S. Army received on Mar. 19, 2026, the H-60Mx Black Hawk Optionally Piloted Vehicle (OPV) at Fort Eustis, Virgin...

Army's autonomous H-60Mx Black Hawk will undergo operational testing with MATRIX software, advancing rotorcraft autonomy for logistics in contested environments.

See The Aviationist →

SpaceX's orbital data center constellation represents a new commercial space infrastructure opportunity that could reshape satellite economics and ground-based connectivity markets.

How Much Does A Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner Cost In 2026?

The 787-9's combination of competitive pricing, advanced efficiency, and proven operational economics continues driving its dominance in the widebody market.

Safety Mar 22, 2026

Why Do Pilots Use The Term "Roger" Instead Of "Yes"?

Roger was one of the first words for 'R' in early aviation phonetic alphabets and it went on to mean 'message received.'

Roger originated as the phonetic alphabet word for 'R' meaning message received, establishing a critical radio communication standard still essential for pilot-ATC clarity today.

See Simple Flying →

The FAA's proactive safety presence at AirVenture demonstrates commitment to general aviation community engagement and real-time hazard mitigation at high-attendance events.

How Quvia helps airlines throw out the IFC playbook and take control

The technology is evolving rapidly, including the antenna hardware. At least five or more broadband Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satell...

Airlines can now leverage Quvia's marketplace to bypass traditional IFC vendors and directly control connectivity solutions as LEO satellite technology accelerates service innovation.

The 787-9's advanced cabin pressurization technology delivers superior passenger comfort on ultra-long-haul routes compared to the 777-200LR.

Commercial Mar 22, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through advanced technologies can simultaneously expand airport capacity and reduce environmental impact as air traffic demand accelerates.

Ukraine appears to have received its first Airborne Early Warning and Control (AEW&C) aircraft, after the Swedish Saab 340 airborne radar wa...

Ukraine's acquisition of the Saab 340 AEW&C aircraft significantly strengthens air defense capabilities against Russian cruise missiles and drone attacks through enhanced situational awareness.

Iranian ballistic missile capability threatens strategic military airbases globally, requiring enhanced air defense systems and operational resilience planning for aviation assets.

NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight

NASA’s quiet supersonic X-59 aircraft made its second flight on Friday, kicking off a series of dozens of test flights in 2026. Although the flight duration was abbreviated due to a technical issue, the team was able to collect information that will inform future tests. “Despite the early landing...

NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic technology demonstrates viability for overcoming regulatory barriers to supersonic commercial aviation through noise reduction innovation.

NASA X-59 Second Flight Ends Early After In-Flight Warning

Test campaign set to continue soon despite in-flight issues.
